definitions.text: Primarily physical senses. To make physical contact with; to bring the hand, finger or other part of the body into contact with. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: Sitting on the bench, the hem of her skirt touched the ground.

definitions.text: To make physical contact with; to bring the hand, finger or other part of the body into contact with. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
To come into (involuntary) contact with; to meet or intersect.
examples: Sitting on the bench, the hem of her skirt touched the ground.

definitions.text: An act of touching, especially with the hand or finger.
examples: Suddenly, in the crowd, I felt a touch at my shoulder.

definitions.text: The faculty or sense of perception by physical contact.
examples: With the lights out, she had to rely on touch to find her desk.

definitions.text: The style or technique with which one plays a musical instrument.
examples: He performed one of Ravel's piano concertos with a wonderfully light and playful touch.
